purpose: Perplexity shift under sentence/word shuffling (Luminol-AIDetect, arXiv:2604.25860 [UNVERIFIED 2026 preprint]). Scores a target and two shuffled variants (sentence-permuted, word-permuted) under a single causal LM and reports 5 features describing the perplexity jump (ppl_sum, ppl_diff, ppl_ratio, ppl_log_ratio, ppl_pct_change). A larger jump means the text's predictability depends MORE on the ordering of its parts — a candidate heuristic signal for human authorship. A structural, order-dependence axis orthogonal in mechanism to Binoculars' cross-perplexity ratio, Fast-DetectGPT's curvature, and DivEye's surprisal moments (those score the natural ordering; this scores permuted variants). Heuristic: the preprint is UNVERIFIED and lit-fiction calibration is empirical, not assumed — no FPR/AUC number is asserted as fact.
use_when: You want a discrimination-evidence signal on a different surface than the natural-ordering probability family (Binoculars / curvature / surprisal moments)
You're hedging against synonym/paraphrase humanizers (word-level substitution does not change order-dependence structure — robustness is an open empirical claim, not assumed)
do_not_use_when: You need a binary AI/human verdict — ships uncalibrated, heuristic, no threshold
The author is ESL / non-native English — non-idiomatic, less order-dependent prose can show a small perplexity delta and FALSELY resemble the AI profile (the GECScore failure mode)

The register is literary fiction and you expect the paper's news-domain FPR — lit-fiction (high human burstiness, long deliberate sentences) calibration is an EMPIRICAL question; the 0.001 FPR claim is UNVERIFIED and not carried as fact
The text may be in the model's training set (memorization biases the perplexities)
You can't install the surprisal tier (transformers + torch)

compute.cost_note: GPT-Neo-2.7B (M2 scorer), ~5.4 GB bf16; 3 forward passes per window (original + sentence-shuffled + word-shuffled); CPU-first verify on ROCm before GPU. M1 is model-free (injected perplexities) and CPU-only.

examples.description: Score with the M2 scorer (gpt_neo_2_7b added behind the CPU-first gate)
